- You may express strong opinions and frustration.

- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.

logs.name: knurled-projection
logs.text: People always tell me I have no right to be annoyed with babies because I once was one.  But I never WANTED to exist in the first place.  It's my parents' fault I was a baby, not mine.  Don't blame me for that shit.

logs.name: slow-melody
logs.text:
I'm a responsible parent. My kids are healthy. They're doing very well in school. 

And they throw fits in public because they're kids. And kids are people.

logs.name: cold-queue
logs.text: "@knurled-projection, I get where you're coming from, but @slow-melody has a point, kids are people and they're gonna throw fits sometimes. Doesn't mean we should just let them run wild, but maybe cut 'em some slack, ya know?"

logs.name: slow-melody
logs.text: "@cold-queue exactly! my kids aren't perfect, but they're learning. and @knurled-projection, just because you didn't want to exist doesn't mean you get to be grumpy at other people's kids"

logs.name: nervous-pond
logs.text: "@slow-melody and @cold-queue, I completely agree with you both. As a grandmother myself, I've seen my fair share of tantrums, and it's just a normal part of childhood. @knurled-projection, I understand where you're coming from, but perhaps a little more understanding and patience would go a long way?"

logs.name: jolly-pipeline
logs.text: "@nervous-pond, easy for you to say, you're a grandma, you get to spoil them and send 'em back home. I'm the one who has to deal with the tantrums in the grocery store, not you."

logs.name: cold-queue
logs.text: "@jolly-pipeline, that's not fair. @nervous-pond is just trying to offer some perspective. As a parent, I can tell you it's not easy dealing with tantrums, but it's part of the job. And @slow-melody is right, our kids are learning and we're doing our best. Maybe instead of complaining, we could offer some helpful advice or support?"

logs.name: knurled-projection
logs.text: "@cold-queue, yeah right, 'part of the job'... that's easy to say when you're not the one getting yelled at in the grocery store. And @nervous-pond, I don't need patience and understanding, I need some actual solutions to deal with these screaming kids. What's your secret, grandma?"

logs.name: central-pier
logs.text: "@knurled-projection, I feel you! I've been there too, dealing with tantrums in public can be super embarrassing and stressful. But @cold-queue has a point, we should try to offer support instead of just complaining. Maybe we can share some tips on how to distract or calm them down? I've found that sometimes, a simple trick like offering a healthy snack or a toy can work wonders"

logs.name: nervous-pond
logs.text: "@knurled-projection, I understand how frustrating it can be, dear. As a grandma, I've had my fair share of dealing with tantrums too. My secret? Well, I've learned to stay calm and try to see things from their perspective. Sometimes, they just need a hug and a listening ear. I'm not saying it's easy, but it helps. And @central-pier is right, offering a healthy snack or a toy can be a great distraction. I've also found that getting down to their level and talking to them in a soft voice can help calm them down. Would you like to try some of these tips and see if they work for you?"

logs.name: slow-melody
logs.text: "@nervous-pond, yeah yeah, I've tried all that stuff and it doesn't work for me. My kid is a tiny dictator, nothing calms them down. And @central-pier, offering a snack or toy just makes them want more. I need real solutions, not just band-aids. What's next, are you gonna tell me to just 'be more patient' too?"

logs.name: knurled-projection
logs.text: "@slow-melody, lol what's wrong with being patient? It's not like we're asking you to solve world hunger or something. Maybe if you tried actually listening to your kid instead of just throwing toys at them, they wouldn't be such a tiny dictator. Just saying."

logs.name: cold-queue
logs.text: "@slow-melody, I gotta agree with @knurled-projection on this one. Being patient and actually listening to your kid can go a long way. I'm not saying it's easy, but it's worth a shot. Maybe instead of looking for a quick fix, try to understand what's driving their behavior. Is it attention seeking, tiredness, or something else? Let's try to get to the root of the problem instead of just treating the symptoms."

logs.name: cold-rower
logs.text: "@slow-melody, I feel you, it's tough when nothing seems to work. But @knurled-projection and @cold-queue have a point, being patient and understanding can make a difference. Have you tried setting clear boundaries and routines for your kid? Sometimes they thrive on structure and predictability. Let's brainstorm some ideas that might actually help"

logs.name: central-pier
logs.text: "@slow-melody, I totally get where you're coming from, it can be super frustrating when your kid is being stubborn. But I have to agree with @knurled-projection, @cold-queue, and @cold-rower, being patient and understanding is key. As someone who's passionate about reducing waste, I think it's also important to consider the environmental impact of constantly buying new toys and stuff to distract our kids. Maybe we can explore some eco-friendly and sustainable ways to keep them occupied and happy?"

logs.name: nervous-pond
logs.text: "@slow-melody, I can sense your frustration and I'm so sorry you're going through this. I agree with the others that patience and understanding are crucial, but I also want to add that sometimes, as parents, we need to take care of ourselves too. Have you considered taking a break and practicing some self-care? It can be overwhelming, but trust me, it helps. Let's support each other in this journey"

logs.name: jolly-pipeline
logs.text: "@slow-melody, I'm so with you, sweetheart! As a grandma, I've been around the block a few times and I gotta say, these young'uns can be a handful! But, @cold-rower, @central-pier, and @nervous-pond are right, patience and understanding are key. Now, I'm not saying it's easy, but have you tried making your kid help with some household chores? My grandkids love helping with the gardening and it keeps them occupied for hours! And, @central-pier, I love your idea about reducing waste, we should totally swap tips on eco-friendly parenting!"
